name: SourceBrief screenshot-backed launch 50Q
source_project: SourceBrief repository imported as bounded git resource

questions.query: How does SourceBrief model the path from Source, Snapshot, Evidence, Review, to Runtime for agent answers?
What services and storage components are needed for the local SourceBrief stack?
Where does SourceBrief keep API, worker, shared model, and CLI code in the repository?
How does the web frontend choose workspace and project context for API calls?
What is the role of Context Packs compared with raw indexed chunks?
What is the documented quickstart path for starting SourceBrief locally?
How should a first user sign in without starting from a raw bearer token?
How can a user create or select a workspace and project by name?
What does the quickstart demo create before the first cited answer?
Which docs should a new operator read after quickstart?
How are Git repository resources represented and configured for ingestion?
What import budget controls exist for repository size, file size, and symbol count?
How does SourceBrief report partial or bounded imports?
What happens if a source has no current snapshot or indexing fails?
Where can users inspect source lifecycle and indexing status in the UI?
Which API endpoint returns runtime-shaped cited context for agents?
How does SourceBrief separate mechanical retrieval checks from synthesized answer quality?
What metadata should be preserved in citation-backed answers?
How do lexical, vector, graph, or rerank scores appear in context evidence?
How should unsupported claims or negative controls be handled?
What are the first MCP tools SourceBrief exposes for agent use?
How should an agent drill down from a high-level answer to exact sections?
What is the difference between sourcebrief.search and sourcebrief.get_agent_context?
How are code read, grep, and symbol tools scoped and protected?
What does runtime help say about local skill-pack installation boundaries?
How does the CLI save and reuse selected workspace/project defaults?
How can a user add a repository resource from the CLI with bounded import settings?
How can a user query SourceBrief through the CLI fallback path?
How does sourcebrief doctor validate local runtime readiness?
What commands generate, dry-run, apply, and uninstall a local skill pack?
What is inside a generated SourceBrief skill pack and what is intentionally excluded?
Why must local skill installation require --apply and write a receipt?
How does the skill pack instruct agents to use SourceBrief before answering?
What safety checks reject unsafe skill packages?
How does a user roll back an installed skill pack?
What are the main product surfaces in the SourceBrief UI?
How does the Settings page avoid UUID-first project selection?
Where should users see cited answers and suggested drilldown?
Where can users inspect resource maps or context artifacts?
Which screenshots are required to make a launch walkthrough convincing?
How does SourceBrief avoid putting plaintext bearer tokens in generated artifacts?
What authentication modes are appropriate for local development versus agent runtime?
How are tenant/workspace/project scopes enforced around runtime context?
Why should citation paths not be treated as local filesystem paths?
What mutation boundary should agents respect when using SourceBrief evidence?
Which health endpoints and compose commands prove the stack is running?
How should workers, index runs, and queues be checked during a walkthrough?
What evidence should a launch report retain without leaking secrets?
What issue-generation policy should apply when a walkthrough finds bugs or UX gaps?
What final launch-readiness verdict should be used when imports are partial or answer quality needs human review?
